text.title: Container Orchestration Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Container Orchestration` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every container MUST set resource.requests AND resource.limits for cpu + memory. Missing either → reject manifest.
rule.rationale: Missing requests = pod gets BestEffort QoS = first to be evicted; missing limits = noisy-neighbor.

rule.statement: Every workload-bearing pod MUST have BOTH liveness + readiness probes wired to a real endpoint (not /). Missing probe → traffic to broken pod.
rule.rationale: Readiness without liveness → permanent unready; liveness without readiness → traffic to startup.

rule.statement: Containers run as a non-root user (`runAsNonRoot: true`, fsGroup set). Root containers rejected at PSS-restricted baseline.
rule.rationale: Root pods amplify compromise blast radius.

rule.statement: Namespace MUST have a default-deny NetworkPolicy with explicit allow rules per service-to-service flow.
Every HPA MUST be paired with a PodDisruptionBudget so scale-down + cluster-upgrade do not violate availability targets.
rule.rationale: Scale events without PDB cause user-visible outages.

rule.statement: Production images MUST be pinned by digest (sha256) — `:latest` and floating tags are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Floating tags break reproducibility and enable supply-chain swaps.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
